title: Anacamptis × lasithica
taxonomy: Anacamptis × lasithica is a hybrid species of flowering plant in the orchid family, Orchidaceae. It is a cross between Anacamptis palustris and Anacamptis morio, both of which are found in the Mediterranean region. As a hybrid, it is not classified into a specific genus or species, but rather as a hybrid name. The classification of Anacamptis × lasithica is generally considered to be a subspecies of Anacamptis morio, with some sources listing it as a distinct species. However, its classification can vary depending on the source and taxonomic authority.

intro: Anacamptis × lasithica, also known as Lasithi orchid, is a hybrid orchid species derived from the cross between Anacamptis papilionacea and Anacamptis palustris. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in wound healing, skin care, and immune system regulation. The bioactive constituents of Anacamptis × lasithica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
